human_actions_pending.action: Complete accessibility verification: Tab through interactive elements, check aria-labels on TitleBar window controls + CombatTrackerTab row trigger, optionally test ARIA live region with NVDA
human_actions_pending.context: 08-07 accessibility code is on master. Details in .planning/phases/08-polish-export-accessibility/08-07-SUMMARY.md

decisions.decision: repairMissingColumns() in migrate.ts rather than relying solely on Drizzle migration 0008
decisions.rationale: Drizzle's migrate() silently skipped migration 0008 even though it wasn't applied to the DB (likely journal hash mismatch or file read issue). The repair function uses PRAGMA table_info — idempotent, guaranteed to run on every startup until columns exist.

decisions.decision: tRPC errorFormatter added to _base.ts to log all procedure errors to main.log
decisions.rationale: Without it, any tRPC procedure throw was completely silent in main.log — the party_size bug was invisible for an entire session. Now errors appear as [tRPC] path: message.

decisions.decision: Null senderFrame handled gracefully in createIPCHandler createContext (allow, don't throw)
decisions.rationale: electron-trpc does NOT wrap createContext in try/catch — a throw causes an unhandled promise rejection and the renderer hangs forever in isLoading. Null senderFrame can't come from an external process; allow it and log if URL fails the check instead.

next_action: 1. Check the running app — does campaigns list load? 2. If yes: complete accessibility verification (Tab nav + aria-labels). 3. Reply 'approved' or note issues. 4. Next session: run /gsd:code-review 8, then spawn gsd-verifier for phase 8, then mark phase 8 complete in STATE.md + ROADMAP.md.
context_notes: Phase 8 is 7/7 plans complete — code all merged to master. The blocker was a schema migration drift bug (party_size missing from campaigns table). Fixed with repairMissingColumns() in migrate.ts + migration 0008. tRPC error logging was the diagnostic that found it. The app is currently running via npm run dev. After the human accessibility checkpoint passes, phase 8 is done and we move to phase 9 (Distribution).
